rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgement Pertaining To Confirming The Final Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 4, 2025. Line 1. PETITIONER DAVID PLEIMANN Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgement Pertaining To Confirming The Final Arbitration Award; is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service for the notice of continued hearing on file. No proof of Service for the Petition to Confirm Arbitration on file. No proof of service for Notice of Motion and Motion for Entry of Judgment Pertaining to Confirming the Final Arbitration Award. Proof of Service filed on October 30, 2025 attests to service of Notice of Motion and Motion for Entr of the Terms of Arbitration. Motion is not supported by Memorandum of Points and Authorities. CRC 3.1112 =(501/AAT) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRLIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS TODD BRABEC AND NANCY GOLDSEN
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 4, 2025. Line 2. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRLIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS TODD BRABEC AND NANCY GOLDSEN per november 05, 2025 order filed. is continued to January 7, 2026. 426 Fillmore Association, a party to this action, responsible for maintenance of common areas. As alleged in the operative complaint "All roofs at 426 Fillmore Street are common area." (paragraph 41). This motion, inter alia, requests the permission for "further inspection, repair, maintenance and or/replacement of the aforementioned roof and ceiling." Therefore, the HOA is ordered to, with Plaintiff's and other defendants' full cooperation, to inspect the roof and the ceiling on a mutually agreeable date on or before December 11, 2025. If parties cannot agree on a date, the inspection shall proceed on December 11, 2025 at 3:30 p.m. Upon the completion of the inspection, HOA shall provide a plan for remediation, if any is necessary (including, but not limited to: timeline for completion; necessary steps to complete the repairs; process for hiring a contractor; party responsible for completing the repairs, etc.) no later than January 5, 2026. Other parties may provide their own plans for remediation no later than January 5, 2026. Supplemental briefs submitted by the parties shall also include any relevant information re: December 17, 2025 deposition of Kevin Kearney. Supplemental briefs shall not exceed 3 pages and shall be accompanied by supporting declarations. =(501/AAT) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
